quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 11 Quiz 5
quiz.subtitle: 11.5: Graphs of Sine and Cosine Functions
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Basic period, amplitude, and key features of sinusoidal graphs

questions.question: Find the period of $y = \sin(2x)$.
questions.explanation: Period = $\frac{2\pi}{|b|} = \frac{2\pi}{2} = \pi$. With $b=2$, the graph completes a full cycle twice as fast.

questions.question: What is the amplitude of the function $y = 3\cos(x)$?
questions.explanation: Amplitude is the absolute value of the coefficient $a$: $|3| = 3$.

questions.question: Find the range of $y = 4\cos(x) - 2$.
questions.explanation: Amplitude $4$, midline at $-2$. Max = $-2 + 4 = 2$. Min = $-2 - 4 = -6$. Range: $[-6, 2]$.

questions.question: What is the amplitude of the function $y = -4\sin(x)$?
Find the period of $y = \cos(4x)$ and the number of complete cycles in $[0, 2\pi]$.
Find the midline of $y = -3\sin(x) + 5$.
questions.explanation: The midline is the vertical shift: $y = 5$. The graph oscillates around this line.

questions.question: Find the maximum value of $y = -2\cos(x) + 3$.
questions.explanation: Maximum occurs when $\cos(x) = -1$ (due to the negative sign). $y = -2(-1) + 3 = 2 + 3 = 5$. Range: $[1, 5]$.

questions.question: The point $(\frac{\pi}{2}, 1)$ is on $y = \sin(x)$. Find the corresponding point on $y = \sin(x - \frac{\pi}{4}) + 2$.
questions.explanation: Phase shift right $\pi/4$: $x = \pi/2 + \pi/4 = 3\pi/4$. Vertical shift up 2: $y = 1 + 2 = 3$. Point: $(3\pi/4, 3)$.

scoreMessages.90: Great work! You have a clear picture of sine and cosine graphs — period, amplitude, midline, range, intercepts, maximum/minimum, and mapping points through transformations.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Key reminders: (1) Period = $2\pi/|b|$, (2) Amplitude = $|a|$, always positive, (3) Midline = $y = k$ (the vertical shift), (4) Range = $[k - |a|, k + |a|]$.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: (1) Finding intercepts by setting $y = 0$ and solving, (2) Maximum value = midline + amplitude, minimum = midline − amplitude, (3) A negative $a$ reflects the graph and swaps where max/min occur, (4) Phase shifts move all points horizontally.
scoreMessages.0: Review sine and cosine graphs: (1) $y = a\sin(bx - h) + k$ — amplitude $|a|$, period $2\pi/|b|$, midline $y = k$, (2) Parent sine starts at $(0,0)$; parent cosine starts at $(0,1)$, (3) Range = $[k - |a|, k + |a|]$, (4) $y$-intercept: set $x = 0$, $x$-intercept: set $y = 0$.
